Title: The GPAT4/6/8 clade functions in Arabidopsis root suberization nonredundantly with the GPAT5/7 clade required for suberin lamellae
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: land plant-specific glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase; acyltransferase with active phosphatase domain
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: aliphatic suberin polyester formation; GPAT4/6/8 clade branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: glycerol-3-phosphate; acyl-CoA substrates of multiple chain lengths
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: acylates glycerol-3-phosphate and, via intrinsic phosphatase activity, produces sn-2 monoacylglycerols used as suberin precursors
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: diffusion barrier for water and mineral relations; protection against salt stress
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes developmentally regulated root suberization and contributes to total aliphatic suberin accumulation; active phosphatase domain is required for full suberin deposition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter reporter analysis; knockout mutant analysis; higher-order mutant analysis; RT-qPCR; fluorol yellow staining; suberin monomer profiling; TEM ultrastructure; native-promoter complementation with wild-type and phosphatase-dead variants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in root endodermis; begins in elongation zone prior to Casparian strip formation; not induced by ABA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: root endodermis; early developmental expression in elongation zone; not ABA inducible under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: abscisic acid treatment; mild salt stress (75 mM NaCl)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GPAT4 is expressed in the Arabidopsis root endodermis before Casparian strip formation. 2. Together with GPAT6 and GPAT8, GPAT4 nonredundantly contributes to root suberin deposition across development. 3. Loss of GPAT4/6/8 clade activity strongly reduces aliphatic suberin monomers of all chain lengths, especially C16 species. 4. Phosphatase-active GPAT4 is required for full rescue of suberin and cutin defects, indicating MAG production contributes to polyester formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: could be used with GPAT5/7 to engineer stronger root suberization and stress protection, but both clades are needed for proper suberin structure
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: gpat4 gpat8 and gpat4 gpat6 gpat8 backgrounds showed reduced growth and required sucrose for seedling establishment; altered polyester deposition may affect development
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: phosphatase-dead GPAT4 partially restored suberin amount/thickness in some contexts but did not fully rescue deposition pattern, indicating catalytic phosphatase activity is important but not the only determinant
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted LC-MS/MS root wax analysis; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter pGPAT4 for complementation and phosphatase-dead constructs

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: land plant-specific glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase; acyltransferase with active phosphatase domain
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: aliphatic suberin polyester formation; GPAT4/6/8 clade branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: glycerol-3-phosphate; acyl-CoA substrates including C16 and longer chains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: acylates glycerol-3-phosphate and, through its active phosphatase domain, generates sn-2 monoacylglycerols that contribute to suberin formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: diffusion barrier for water and mineral relations; protection against salt stress
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: contributes to broad suberin monomer accumulation, with a stronger effect on C16 monomers; required with GPAT4/8 for normal suberization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter reporter analysis; knockout mutant analysis; higher-order mutant analysis; fluorol yellow staining; suberin monomer profiling; TEM ultrastructure; complementation with wild-type and phosphatase-dead GPAT6
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in root endodermis; begins in late differentiation zone; not induced by ABA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: root endodermis in late differentiation zone; not ABA inducible under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: abscisic acid treatment; mild salt stress (75 mM NaCl)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GPAT6 is expressed in the root endodermis and contributes to suberization. 2. Combined loss with GPAT4 and GPAT8 causes a major drop in total aliphatic suberin monomers. 3. The mutant profile suggests relatively strong contribution to C16 suberin monomers. 4. A phosphatase-active GPAT6 is needed for full polyester deposition, although phosphatase-dead GPAT6 can partially alter suberin structure in gpat5 gpat7.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful in engineering suberin amount, especially with GPAT4/8 and GPAT5/7, but alone insufficient for proper lamellation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: defects in GPAT6-containing mutant combinations contribute to reduced suberization and altered barrier properties
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: phosphatase-dead GPAT6 did not rescue suberin amount in gpat6 but could partially rescue suberin thickness or alter ultrastructure in gpat5 gpat7
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted LC-MS/MS root wax analysis; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter pGPAT6 for complementation and phosphatase-dead constructs

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: land plant-specific glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase; acyltransferase with active phosphatase domain
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: aliphatic suberin polyester formation; GPAT4/6/8 clade branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: glycerol-3-phosphate; acyl-CoA substrates of multiple chain lengths
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: acylates glycerol-3-phosphate and uses an active phosphatase domain to generate sn-2 monoacylglycerols that contribute to suberin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: diffusion barrier for water and mineral relations; protection against salt stress
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes root suberization together with GPAT4 and GPAT6; phosphatase activity contributes to full suberin deposition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter reporter analysis; knockout mutant analysis; higher-order mutant analysis; fluorol yellow staining; suberin monomer profiling; TEM ultrastructure; complementation with wild-type and phosphatase-dead GPAT8
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in root endodermis; begins in elongation zone prior to Casparian strip formation; not induced by ABA in reporter analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: root endodermis; early developmental expression in elongation zone; largely not ABA inducible in wild type
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: abscisic acid treatment; mild salt stress (75 mM NaCl)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GPAT8 is expressed in the root endodermis before Casparian strip formation. 2. Together with GPAT4 and GPAT6, it supports developmentally regulated suberin deposition. 3. Phosphatase-dead GPAT8 only partially complements some suberin defects, supporting a role for its active phosphatase domain. 4. In gpat5 gpat7, ABA caused only modest GPAT8 induction and limited globular suberin deposition remained.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential component for engineering root barrier strength when combined with GPAT5/7; phosphatase state affects product structure
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss of GPAT8 with GPAT4/6 contributes to reduced growth and stress sensitivity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: phosphatase-dead GPAT8 occasionally improved continuity/partial lamellation in gpat5 gpat7, implying that reduced phosphatase activity favors lamellar organization
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted LC-MS/MS root wax analysis; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter pGPAT8 for complementation and phosphatase-dead constructs

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: land plant-specific glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase; acyltransferase with inactive phosphatase domain
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: aliphatic suberin polyester formation; GPAT5/7 clade branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: glycerol-3-phosphate; predominantly C18 and very-long-chain acyl-CoA substrates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: acylates glycerol-3-phosphate to form lysophosphatidic acids because its phosphatase domain is inactive
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root endodermis; root cortex after ABA induction; root periderm
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: diffusion barrier for water and mineral relations; protection against salt stress
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for target product and required for proper lamellar organization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for ABA-responsive suberization and for normal lamellated suberin ultrastructure; especially promotes incorporation of C18-C24 monomers including strong effects on C22 species
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter reporter analysis; RT-qPCR; single and double knockout analysis; fluorol yellow staining; suberin monomer profiling; TEM ultrastructure; phosphatase inhibitor experiments; comparison with phosphatase-dead GPAT4/6/8 transgenes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in root endodermis beginning in late differentiation zone; strongly induced and spatially expanded by ABA, including ectopic cortex/epidermis expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: root endodermis late differentiation zone; ABA-inducible; ectopic expression in cortex and epidermis after ABA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: abscisic acid treatment; mild salt stress (75 mM NaCl); phosphatase inhibitor treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GPAT5 is an endodermis-expressed, ABA-inducible suberin biosynthetic GPAT. 2. GPAT5/7 double knockout causes about 50% reduction in aliphatic suberin, major depletion of C22 monomers, and replacement of lamellated suberin by amorphous globular deposits. 3. GPAT5 is crucial for ABA-regulated cortical/endodermal suberization and salt protection. 4. Its inactive phosphatase domain supports LPA formation that is implicated in suberin lamellae formation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for ABA-regulated suberization and lamellae formation within GPAT branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong candidate for engineering root suberin and salinity protection, but must be coordinated with GPAT4/6/8 for correct polymer amount and structure
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss causes delayed suberization, altered ultrastructure, reduced growth under salt stress; excessive manipulation could alter barrier properties and nutrient uptake
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GPAT5/7 clade lacks active phosphatase domains; when this branch is absent, active-phosphatase GPATs alone generate globular rather than lamellated suberin
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted LC-MS/MS root wax analysis; RT-qPCR

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: land plant-specific glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase; acyltransferase with inactive phosphatase domain
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: aliphatic suberin polyester formation; GPAT5/7 clade branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: glycerol-3-phosphate; C18 and very-long-chain acyl-CoA substrates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: acylates glycerol-3-phosphate to produce lysophosphatidic acids because the phosphatase domain is inactive
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root endodermis; root cortex after ABA induction; root periderm
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: diffusion barrier for water and mineral relations; protection against salt stress
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for target product and required for proper lamellar organization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: functions with GPAT5 in ABA-responsive suberization, contributes additively to C20/C22 monomer incorporation, and is essential for lamellated suberin structure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter reporter analysis; RT-qPCR; single and double knockout analysis; fluorol yellow staining; suberin monomer profiling; TEM ultrastructure; phosphatase inhibitor comparison
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in root endodermis beginning in late differentiation zone; strongly induced and spatially expanded by ABA similar to GPAT5
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: root endodermis late differentiation zone; ABA inducible; ectopic expression in cortex and epidermis after ABA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: abscisic acid treatment; mild salt stress (75 mM NaCl); phosphatase inhibitor treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GPAT7 is an endodermis-expressed close homolog of GPAT5 that also contributes to root suberin formation. 2. GPAT5 and GPAT7 act additively on C20/C22 suberin monomers and together are required for normal suberin content. 3. GPAT5/7 loss abolishes the normal continuous lamellated suberin layer and produces globular amorphous deposits. 4. GPAT7 is highly induced by ABA and can compensate in some mixed-clade mutant backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for ABA-regulated suberization and lamellae formation within GPAT branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable target to increase inducible root suberin and salt tolerance in combination with GPAT5 and GPAT4/6/8
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss causes delayed suberization and increased salt sensitivity in combination with GPAT5
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GPAT7 shares 88% amino acid identity with GPAT5 and appears partially compensatory in some mutant backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted LC-MS/MS root wax analysis; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter reporter used for expression analysis
